title: DNS issues with bridge networks
body:
## Setup
podman 5.4.2 on Debian 13

Run as root.

```
podman create network1
podman create network2
podman create --driver macvlan ... network3
```

## Test Cases

`podman run --network network1 --network network2 alpine cat /etc/resolv.conf`
shows two resolvers, OK

`podman run --network network1 --dns 1.1.1.1 alpine cat /etc/resolv.conf`
shows only resolvers from `network1`, not OK

`podman run --network network3 alpine cat /etc/resolv.conf`
shows resolvers from host, OK

`podman run --network network1 --network network3 alpine cat /etc/resolv.conf`
shows only resolvers from `network1`, not OK

`podman run --network network3 --dns 1.1.1.1 alpine cat /etc/resolv.conf`
shows only `1.1.1.1`, OK

```
podman network update network1 --dns-add 1.1.1.1

# verifies 1.1.1.1 is added
podman network inspect network1

podman run --network network1 alpine cat /etc/resolv.conf
# does not show 1.1.1.1, not OK

podman network update network1 --dns-drop 1.1.1.1
```

## Background
I want to put a few containers into an internal bridge network, such that they can find each other by names.
Meanwhile, one container needs internet access, so I put it into another macvlan network.
IP addresses works, but the container only sees the DNS server from the bridge network, so it cannot access Internet

comments.body: @coolwanglu in general, we really prefer that when you report issues upstream, you test with a much more recent version to ensure this is not something we have not already fixed.  Please consider that.

@baude good point. I tested on podman 5.6.1 on a recent CoreOS, 

The first issue is still reproducible, but it can be explained by https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/17499

The second issue cannot be reproduced.

So I'm closing it.

> ## Настраивать
> podman 5.4.2 в Debian 13
> 
> Запускать как root.
> 
> ```
> podman create network1
> podman create network2
> podman create --driver macvlan ... network3
> ```
> 
> ## Тестовые случаи
> `podman run --network network1 --network network2 alpine cat /etc/resolv.conf` показывает два резольвера, ОК
> 
> `podman run --network network1 --dns 1.1.1.1 alpine cat /etc/resolv.conf` показывает только резолверы из `network1`, не ОК
> 
> `podman run --network network3 alpine cat /etc/resolv.conf` показывает резолверы с хоста, ОК
> 
> `podman run --network network1 --network network3 alpine cat /etc/resolv.conf` показывает только резолверы из `network1`, не ОК
> 
> `podman run --network network3 --dns 1.1.1.1 alpine cat /etc/resolv.conf` показывает только `1.1.1.1`, ОК
> 
> ```
> podman network update network1 --dns-add 1.1.1.1
> 
> # verifies 1.1.1.1 is added
> podman network inspect network1
> 
> podman run --network network1 alpine cat /etc/resolv.conf
> # does not show 1.1.1.1, not OK
> 
> podman network update network1 --dns-drop 1.1.1.1
> ```
> 
> ## Фон
> Я хочу объединить несколько контейнеров во внутреннюю сеть типа «мост», чтобы они могли находить друг друга по именам. При этом одному контейнеру нужен доступ в интернет, поэтому я помещаю его в другую сеть MacVLAN. IP-адреса работают, но контейнер видит только DNS-сервер из сети типа «мост», поэтому не может получить доступ в интернет.
